name: YourMoney Active Pension Members

columns.name: CURRENT_EMPLOYER_SALARY_AMT

columns.name: VETERAN_STATUS
columns.description: The veteran status of the member for pension purposes.

columns.name: SERVICE_YEARS_QTY
columns.description: The full years of service in the pension fund credited to the member.

columns.name: LOCATION_CODE
columns.description: The code that identifies the location of the employer reporting at the end of the reporting period.

columns.name: 20_YEAR_STATUS
columns.description: The status that indicates whether the member is exempt from Chapter 78. “YES” indicates the member attained 20 years of service before the Chapter 78 effective date (June 28, 2012) and is exempt from allowance-based health benefit premium calculations if the member retires with at least 25 years of service. All other members are “NO”.

columns.name: MEMBER_LAST_NAME
columns.description: The last name of the member at the end of the reporting period.

columns.name: MEMBER_MI
columns.description: The middle initial of the member at the end of the reporting period.

columns.name: ENROLLMENT_DATE
columns.description: The date that the member enrolled in the pension fund.

columns.name: AS_OF_DATE
columns.description: The last day of the four quarters of data represented in this dataset.

columns.name: MEMBER_FIRST_NAME
columns.description: The first name of the member at the end of the reporting period.

columns.name: EMPLOYER_NAME
columns.description: The name of the employer reporting at the end of the reporting period.

columns.name: LOCATION_NAME
columns.description: The county in which the employer reporting at the end of the reporting period is located, or REGIONAL, or STATEWIDE.

columns.name: PENSION_FUND_ID
columns.description: The identifier of the pension fund for which this data is being reported.

columns.name: PENSION_FUND_NAME
columns.description: The name of the pension fund for which this data is being reported.

columns.name: PENSION_GROUP_NAME
columns.description: The name of the pension subgroup if applicable, or NOT APPLICABLE.

columns.name: SERVICE_MONTHS_QTY
columns.description: The months of service in the pension fund for the most recent partial year credited to the member.

columns.name: TOTAL_MONTHS_QTY
columns.description: The total months of service in the pension fund credited to the member (where each full year of service equals twelve months).

columns.name: ALL_EMPLOYERS_SALARY_AMT
columns.description: The total salary eligible for pension credit reported by all employers for all locations during the most recent four quarters.

columns.name: 25_YEAR_DATE
columns.description: The date upon which a member achieved twenty-five years of pension service, otherwise blank.

columns.name: MEMBERSHIP_TIER
columns.description: The category of retirement benefits available based on the pension fund enrollment date of the member.

description: The data file reflects data for employees who are active members in a state pension system as reported by the employer. It lists the salary eligible for pension credit for the four most recent calendar quarters reported as well as attributes that describe the status of the member for pension purposes. Additional information is in the attached dataset summary PDF (available on the [About] tab under "Attachments").

columns.name: RECORD_TYPE
columns.description: Identifies master or detail record for an employee.

columns.name: EMPLOYEE_RELATIONS_GROUP
columns.description: Classification indicating whether employee is aligned or not aligned with a union and what type of work that unit performs. This grouping determines what salary and benefits the employee receives. This is the employee relations group associated with the employee at the end of the reporting period.

columns.name: CASH_IN_LIEU_MAINTENANCE
columns.description: Contractual biweekly payment for State Police Employee Relations Groups.

columns.name: SUPPLEMENTAL_PAY
columns.description: Wages paid to an employee that were not included in regular pay. In some existing state contracts, supplemental payments for overtime are required. Includes lump sum payments of unused vacation leave, compensatory time, and personal leave bank time.

columns.name: CALENDAR_YEAR
columns.description:
Four digit year representing the twelve month period starting with
January and ending with December. Indicates the calendar year in
which payments were actually made to the employee. Calendar year
and calendar quarter together represent the end of the reporting
period.

columns.name: LAST_NAME
columns.description: Last name of the employee at the end of the reporting period.

columns.name: CLOTHING_UNIFORM_PAYMENTS
columns.description: Wages paid to eligible employees for clothing and uniform purchase and maintenance.

columns.name: OVERTIME_PAYMENTS
columns.description: Overtime wages.

columns.name: LUMP_SUM_PAY
columns.description: Wages paid to an employee upon retirement for any unused sick leave accrued. The maximum payout is $15,000 per employee.

columns.name: YTD_EARNINGS
columns.description: Sum of all payments for the employee by paid_department_agency_desc and paid_section_desc during the reporting period.

columns.name: REGULAR_PAY
columns.description: Wages earned by an employee for working a predetermined number of hours or days in a week as dictated by the employee’s title.

columns.name: ONE_TIME_PAYMENTS
columns.description: Wages paid as bonus and one-time payments or shift differential for employees working night or evening shifts.

columns.name: FIRST_NAME
columns.description:
First name of the employee at the end of the reporting period. The
literal ‘none’ has been used when there is no first name on record.

columns.name: PAYROLL_ID
columns.description:
Randomly generated number that helps pair a master record with its
detail record(s).

columns.name: CALENDAR_QUARTER
columns.description:
Payroll data will be published quarterly. The single digit calendar
quarter represents the end of the period that the data spans and not
the actual quarter that the payment was made. For example, when
calendar quarter is set to ‘2’, the data would include payments made
from January through June for the given calendar year. Calendar
year and calendar quarter together represent the end of the
reporting period.

columns.description: The sum of overtime wages for one or more departments/agencies by an employee during the reporting period.

columns.name: MASTER_YTD_REGULAR_PAY
columns.description: The sum of wages for one or more departments/agencies that are earned by an employee for working a predetermined number of hours or days in a week as dictated by the employee’s title during the reporting period.

columns.name: MASTER_TITLE_DESC
columns.description: The title is a descriptive name that identifies a position or group of positions with similar duties, responsibilities and qualifications. This is the title associated with the employee at the end of the reporting period.

columns.name: MASTER_SECTION_DESC
columns.description: The area of the department to which the employee is assigned at the end of the reporting period.

columns.name: MASTER_DEPARTMENT_AGENCY_DESC
columns.description: Discrete organization within the State of New Jersey associated with the employee at the end of the reporting period.

columns.name: SALARY_HOURLY_RATE
columns.description: Depending on the value in the column Compensation Method, the value in this column is either the annual salary, hourly rate or a daily rate for the employee at the end of the reporting period. columns.description: Middle initial of the employee at the end of the reporting period.

columns.name: EXPENDED_AMT
columns.description: The amount expended or spent either to external payees or internally within the state government.

columns.name: YTD_AMT
columns.description: The sum of the Obligation Amt and Expended Amt fields for this record.

columns.name: EXPENDITURE_DESC
columns.description: Further describes the purpose of the expenditure or obligation within the appropriation.

columns.name: IBNO_AGENCY
columns.description: The State Agency that rec’d the funding and subsequently made the expenditure or created the obligation.

columns.name: EXPENDITURE_CATEGORY
columns.description: A high level description identifying the purpose of the obligation or expenditure.

columns.name: STATEWIDE_PROGRAM
columns.description: A description for the categorization of the many separate programs across state government. Can combine multiple funds, organizations, and agencies for reporting purposes.

columns.name: FUNDING_CATEGORY
columns.description: The source of the funding used for spending, as categorized similar to the Appropriation Act.

columns.name: BUDGET_FISCAL_YEAR
columns.description: The period of time in which funding was provided as per the Appropriation Act signed into law. Although all state funded appropriations have an end date of July 31st of the subsequent year, appropriations with a federal funding stay open based on the grant period. Obligations stay open past any stipulated appropriation end date until paid or cancelled.

columns.name: FY_THROUGH_DATE
columns.description: The last day of the month of data represented in this dataset.

columns.name: ORGANIZATION_NAME
columns.description: The appropriation level organization name that received the funding and subsequently made the expenditure or created the obligation.

columns.name: PAYEE_NAME
columns.description: The payee or recipient of the spending. This field may be blank if the expenditure or obligation was generated for internal purposes.

columns.name: OBLIGATION_AMT
columns.description: This is the balance of all encumbrances for this record. Also known as an encumbrance, an obligation is an intention to expend funds in the future for a specific purpose (traditional procurement, a grant, state-aid, etc) and to a specific payee.

description: The data file reflects data for a budget fiscal year as a cumulatively fiscal year-to-date total. For ease of reporting and to facilitate understanding of the data, the expenditures are categorized by department/agency, expenditure description, organization, statewide program, funding category, expenditure category, and payee. Note that the 12 accounting months begin in July and run sequentially through the 12th period which stays open for both calendar months June and July of the following year
